methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $66M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 7 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series A), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Chef Robotics is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $65.6M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of nine comparable companies—one public entity and eight private firms operating in the same sector at equivalent or adjacent stages. Key private comparables include Foundation Robotics, 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, and Locus Robotics, with post-money valuations ranging from $500M to $10.0B.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $66M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, yielding $131M. Peer-median analysis across the seven most relevant same-sector comparables identifies a median valuation of $1.6B, with a range of $300M to $10.0B. The peer set includes Foundation Robotics at $3.0B (Series A), Bedrock Robotics at $1.8B (Series B), and Dexterity at $1.6B (Series B).

The two methods produce a triangulated range of $112M to $1.9B, with a median of $891M. The wide dispersion reflects meaningful uncertainty in comparability: peers span multiple sub-stages within Series A/B and exhibit significant heterogeneity in maturity and capital efficiency. Stage-progression suggests a floor near current cash deployment, while peer-median anchors upside to established market valuations. Resolution of valuation within this range depends on Chef Robotics' demonstrated progress on unit economics, customer traction, and technical differentiation relative to the peer cohort.
